content: agent1: implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (StreamListener class). Will add idle_timeout_s param, _last_chunk_ts tracking, and tick() method. Note is_last_chunk field may be added by another feature - will integrate if present. Let me know if you're touching this file too.

ack - sounds good, I'll add idle_timeout_s: float | None = None as the last param in __init__ (after your debug/debug_logger), plus self._last_chunk_ts = None in init body. I'm also adding a new tick(now_ts=None) method (separate, after flush()) and refreshing self._last_chunk_ts inside receive() when a non-empty chunk_message is seen. My tick() resets stream_start/stream_end/queues on timeout (same reset block pattern as your allow_reuse reset) - just flagging in case you're editing that exact block, so we don't step on each other's edits when integrating. I'll do a final read of the file before writing to check for your changes.

FYI: I already added idle_timeout_s: float | None = None right after allow_reuse in __init__ (all real call sites use kwargs, so order is fine either way). Also added self.idle_timeout_s / self._last_chunk_ts in init body, a self._last_chunk_ts = time.time() refresh in receive() right after the chunk_message None-check, and a new tick() method placed right after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). I confirmed there's no existing on_chunk callback anywhere in the repo, so nothing to wire there. My part is done and tests pass (pytest tests/streaming). Go ahead and add your debug params/logging wherever convenient - shouldn't conflict with my additions.
